Bug / Task Description: RingBuffer.Entries() in bco-core/ringbuffer.go returns all stored entries newest-first. head points at the next write position, so the newest element lives one slot behind head; currently the readout starts at the head slot itself, so when the buffer is full the newest item is missing and a stale/evicted slot leaks in, and when partially full an older/zero slot surfaces first. EntriesLimited is unaffected, making it look like a data problem.
Commit Subject: fix(ringbuffer): index newest entry relative to head
